title: Gov. Parnell names ANHC’s Annette Evans Smith to Alaska Native Language Council
summary: Gov. Sean Parnell appointed Stephen Walkie Charles, Delores Churchill, April Gale Laktonen Councellor, Bernadette Yaayuk Alvanna-Stimpfle and Alaska Native Heritage Center (ANHC) President and CEO Annette Evans Smith to the Alaska Native Language Preservation and Advisory Council. title: In memory, October/November 2013
summary: Axel J. Beltz, 51 Axel J. Beltz passed away July 31 at the Alaska Native Medical Center in Anchorage. Mr. Beltz was born Aug. 18, 1960, in Anchorage. He is survived by his wife, Donna Beltz; daughter, Bree Avellaneda; mother, Arne Beltz; and brother, William Beltz. Walter G. Buretz, 82 Walter G. Buretz passed away April 10 at the Washington County Health Center in Washington, Pa. Mr. Buretz was born in Feb. 1930, in Brownsville, Pa. He is survived by his son, Daniel W. Buretz, and friend, Emma Colborn. Douglas B. Coven, 61 Douglas B. Coven passed away Aug. 15 in Eustis, Fla. Mr. Cove...

title: CIHA celebrates grand opening of Loussac Place
summary: Cook Inlet Housing Authority (CIHA) celebrated the official grand opening of Loussac Place on Oct. 3. CIHA’s latest development, located in mid-town Anchorage. title: ANHC receives Governor’s Award
summary: The Alaska Native Heritage Center (ANHC) was selected as a 2012 recipient of the Governor’s Awards for the Arts and Humanities for its distinguished service to the humanities. summary: The winners for the August, September and October 2012 Stock Will Participation Prizes are Kevin Ray Andersen, Robin Ann Murphy and Burt Shaginoff, respectively. summary: CIRI Land Development Co. (CLDC) and its subsidiary, Pacific Tower Properties Inc. (PTP), are pleased to announce the launch of their new websites. title: Fire Island photo contest winner
summary: Lee Hamerski of Anchorage submitted a winning entry to CIRI and the Renewable Energy Alaska Project’s Fire Island Photo Contest, and won a trip for two to Fire Island. The post Fire Island photo contest winner first appeared on CIRI .

title: Dividend, Elders’ payment news
summary: CIRI will mail or directly deposit the fourth quarter 2012 dividend of $9.93 per share on Dec. 7. Shareholders who own 100 shares of stock will receive $993. title: CIRI Golf Classic is a hole-in-one for local nonprofits
summary: Continuing its tradition of commitment to the community, CIRI hosted the 29th annual CIRI Golf Classic in August. summary: CIRI shareholder Jack Razo was hired as an assistant property manager with Pacific Tower Properties Management Inc., a CIRI company. title: Descendant graduates
summary: CIRI descendant and The CIRI Foundation recipient Joel Isaak graduated from the University of Alaska Fairbanks with a bachelor of fine arts in sculpture on May 13. title: A word from the president:  AFN needs to evolve to better serve, unify
summary: By: Margie Brown, CIRI president and chief executive officer A year ago, I wrote about the Alaska Federation of Natives’ (AFN) progress toward becoming once again a vibrant, innovative organization that effectively represents the collective, yet broad interests of Alaska Native people. title: CIRI recognizes CIRI Shareholder, Elder Shareholder of the Year
summary: CIRI selected B. Agnes Brown as the 2012 Shareholder of the Year. summary: CIRI hosted an economic development summit on Sept. 24 at the BP Energy Center.
